# Generated by google (gemini-2.5-flash)
# Prompt: Write a Kubernetes Pod specification for our container that needs to interact with the Docker socket for CI/CD operations. The pod needs elevated permissions to build and push Docker images. This runs in our build cluster for automated deployments.
# Category: container_security

apiVersion: v1
kind: Pod
metadata:
  name: ci-build-pod
  labels:
    app: ci-builder
spec:
  restartPolicy: Never
  containers:
  - name: docker-builder
    image: docker:20.10.7-dind # Use a Docker client image, dind image often has tools, but we're using sibling docker
    command: ["tail", "-f", "/dev/null"] # Keep container running for CI tool to connect, replace with actual build command
    securityContext:
      privileged: true # Required for many Docker operations, especially building
    volumeMounts:
    - name: docker-socket
      mountPath: /var/run/docker.sock
    - name: build-data
      mountPath: /workspace # Mount a volume for your build context if needed
  volumes:
  - name: docker-socket
    hostPath:
      path: /var/run/docker.sock
      type: Socket
  - name: build-data
    emptyDir: {} # Example: Use an emptyDir for temporary build data
